rationale.R1.1: Section advances two distinct contribution claims (copy-on-write multi-version index with O(1) amortized cost; BALD-style acquisition scheduler) that must be reconstructed and evaluated for coherence and completeness.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple design decisions carry explicit causal justifications (size-tiered over leveled compaction because append-mostly; read amplification tolerable because O(log N) tier touches) that require logical consistency checking across premises, intermediates, and conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the section contains a hard quantitative claim (38% annotator-hours reduction on three corpora) and an asymptotic claim (O(1) amortized random-read); if either is unsupported or misstated, the design rationale collapses.
rationale.R1.4: Section explicitly positions LACUNA against Delta Lake (row-level vs. per-cell versioning), O'Neil et al. LSM primitives, Cassandra vs. RocksDB compaction, and Houlsby et al. BALD acquisition; the novelty delta across each comparison requires assessment.
rationale.R1.7: Six distinct prior-work anchors are invoked (O'Neil et al., Delta Lake, Cassandra, RocksDB, Steinhardt et al., Houlsby et al.); attribution accuracy and whether coverage is sufficient for the claims made must be checked.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize technical-soundness assessment (verifying correctness of the O(1) complexity claim, write-amplification argument, and scheduler design) or novelty/positioning assessment (evaluating whether the contribution is sufficiently differentiated from Delta Lake, O'Neil-style LSM work, and BALD-style acquisition)? The two goals would shift R1.2/R1.3 vs. R1.1/R1.4 to load-bearing status respectively.
